folio.label: 79r
folio.l: For goddes loue , as chese a newe requeste
Take al my good , and late my body goo
Nay thanne q¶ she , I shrewe vs bothe twoo
For thougú that I be foul , old and pore
I nold for al the metal , ne for ore
That vnder erthe is graue , or litú a boue
But if thy wyf I were , and eke thy loue
¢ My loue q¶ he , nay my dampnacione
Allas that ony , of my nacione
Shulde euäe so foule , des¹aged be
But al for nougút , the ende is this , that he
Constreyned was , he nedes must hire wedde
And taketú this old wyf , & gotú to bedde
Now wolde som~e men seyn , ¹auenture
That for my negligence , I do no cure
To telle ©ow the ioye , and al the array
That at the fest was , that ilke day
To whicú thyngö , shortly answeren I shal
I seye there was no feest , ne ioye at al
There nas but heuynesse , and mechel sorwe
For preuyly he wedded hire , on the morwe
And al day after , hid hi~ as an Owle
So woo was hi~ , his wyf loked so foule
Gret was the woo , the knygút had in his thougút
Whan he was witú his wyf , abedde I brougút

note.notetxt: 1057 ink becomes clearer, pause in writing?ODR

folio.l: He waluetú . and he turnetú to and froo
This olde wyf , lay smylyng euäe moo
And seide , o dere husbonde benedicite
Faretú euäy knygút thus , witú his wyf as ©e
Is this the lawe , of kyngö Arthoures hous
Is euäy knygút of his , thus daungerous
I am ©oure owen loue , & eke ©oure wyf´
I am she , whicú æat saued hatú , ©oure lyf´
And certes , ©et ne dide I ©ow , neuäe vnrigút
Why fare ©e thus witú me , this first nygút
ñe faren like a man , had lost his wytte
What is my gilt , for goddes loue telle itte
And it shal ben amended , if I may
Amendid q¶ this knygút , allas nay nay
It wol not ben amended , neuäe moo
Thow art so lotúly , and so old also
And ther to comen , of so lowe a kynde
That lytel wonder is , thougú I walwe & wynde
So wolde god , myn hert wold brest
Is this q¶ she , the cause of ©oure vnrest
ñee certeynly q¶ he , no wonder is
